title: Low Disk Space Cleanup Triage
description: Diagnose low disk space, identify safe cleanup targets, run only approved cleanup actions, then verify recovered space.
starter_prompt: Diagnose low disk space and prepare a safe cleanup plan before deleting anything.

phases.prompt: Define cleanup scope. Treat user files, app data, and browser profiles as preserve-by-default unless explicitly approved.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; Section 'Volumes'; Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,HealthStatus,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-Table -AutoSize; Section 'Common cleanup locations'; $paths=@($env:TEMP, "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Temp", "$env:USERPROFILE\Downloads", "$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop"); foreach($p in $paths){ if(Test-Path -LiteralPath $p){ $m=Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum; [pscustomobject]@{Path=$p;Items=$m.Count;Bytes=[int64]$m.Sum} } }; Section 'Windows cleanup candidates'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath "$env:SystemRoot\Temp" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object -Property Length -Sum | ForEach-Object { [pscustomobject]@{Path="$env:SystemRoot\Temp";Items=$_.Count;Bytes=[int64]$_.Sum} }; Section 'Recycle bin estimate'; Get-CimInstance Win32_LogicalDisk | Select-Object DeviceID,FreeSpace,Size | Format-Table -AutoSize

phases.prompt: Collect read-only free-space and candidate-location evidence. Do not delete files in this phase.
phases.completion: Space pressure and candidate cleanup targets are visible.

phases.prompt: Analyze cleanup targets. Separate safe temp cleanup from user-file review items. Include confidence and any missing evidence.
phases.completion: Safe and review-required targets are separated.

phases.prompt: Ask for explicit user approval before cleanup. Offer restore point handling when system locations or admin cleanup are involved.
phases.completion: Approval and restore point decision are recorded.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; $cutoff=(Get-Date).AddDays(-7); $targets=@($env:TEMP, "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Temp"); foreach($t in $targets){ if(Test-Path -LiteralPath $t){ Write-Output ('== Cleaning old temp files: ' + $t + ' =='); Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $t -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | Remove-Item -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue } }; Write-Output 'Approved temp cleanup complete. User files, Downloads, Desktop, and browser profiles were not deleted.'

phases.prompt: Run only user-approved temp cleanup after approval and restore point offer. Never delete user documents or downloads automatically.
phases.completion: Approved cleanup completed or failure is captured.

phases.prompt: Verify free-space state after approved cleanup and note whether more manual review is needed.
Verify cleanup did not overreach. Check that evidence supports actions and that user data was not targeted.

Create a technician and customer-ready handoff report with evidence, findings, actions/tools used, verification status, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and prioritized follow-up next actions.
Keep logs and reports. Remove only workflow-created scratch files.

acceptance_criteria: Read-only disk-space evidence is collected before cleanup.
Cleanup actions run only after explicit approval.
Final report lists evidence, actions, cleanup status, risks, and follow-up items.
